tips_en: I'd recommend reading the NatureBench paper first, as it provides the most thought-provoking findings: even Claude Opus 4.7 only surpasses SOTA on 17.8% of real scientific tasks, with failures dominated by wrong method choice—setting a sobering baseline that contextualizes the other two papers. The terminal-agent and AOHP papers both address agent capability and efficiency (training data synthesis, OS-level agent design) but from complementary angles: CLI-Universe focuses on building better training data for agents, while AOHP redesigns the OS substrate itself. Together, the three papers form a coherent picture of the current agent frontier—evaluation, training, and deployment infrastructure.
tips_zh: 建议先阅读 NatureBench 论文，因为其结论最发人深省：即便是 Claude Opus 4.7，在真实科学任务上超越 SOTA 的比例也仅有 17.8%，且失败主要源于方法选择错误——这一基线为另外两篇论文提供了重要参照。终端代理和 AOHP 论文都从不同角度关注智能体能力与效率（训练数据合成、操作系统级智能体设计），互为补充：CLI-Universe 聚焦于构建更好的智能体训练数据，AOHP 则重新设计操作系统底层。三篇论文共同勾勒出当前智能体前沿的全貌——评估、训练与部署基础设施。
